title: podman events format json
body:
Enable podman events to format the output as jsonlines
close #3159 

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: this function needs to be implemented for the local and remote client.  i believe you have only done the local client.
Podman events man page needs to be updated, as well as an example.
Implemented for the remote client and changes the man page. @baude @rhatdan  PTAL
@QiWang19 would it be possible to add a integration test to protect against regression?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *QiWang19* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I saw this error `[+0658s] time="2019-06-05T17:17:58Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket""`  is it related with the skip? did I check the `info.Distribution != "fedora"` right?

> Overall LGTM. It would be great to extend the tests to also check the content of the output. Some values might be different, but we could check for the keys.

I agree., this gives us a chance to test for specific events too, which is nice
